event_description: A helicopter is tracked from a high angle while sitting stationary on an airport tarmac. The EO view clearly shows the main rotor blades spinning, creating motion blur. The IR view reveals a highly concentrated, bright white (hot) thermal spot on top of the fuselage, directly beneath the rotor mast, corresponding to the active engine exhaust.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with rotor motion without ground translation; the tarmac background nearby
options.B: the object with the tarmac background; rotor motion without ground translation nearby
options.C: the object with apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; runway, taxiway, or apron markings nearby
options.D: the object with runway, taxiway, or apron markings; camera orbit or scale change nearby

qa.evidence_note: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which fixed-state cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: the tarmac background; view scale or angle changes
options.B: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; view scale or angle changes
options.C: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; view scale or angle changes
options.D: rotor motion without ground translation; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry
options.B: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; the tarmac background
options.C: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; rotor motion without ground translation
options.D: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; runway, taxiway, or apron markings

qa.evidence_note: a concentrated hotspot beneath the rotor mast marks active engine exhaust

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; near the object
options.B: rotor motion without ground translation; near the object
options.C: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; near the object
options.D: the tarmac background; near the object

qa.evidence_note: treating rotor activity as taxiing or relocation

qa.question: Which EO/IR cue pair is visible?

options.A: EO: rotor motion without ground translation; IR: runway, taxiway, or apron markings
options.B: EO: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.C: EO: the tarmac background; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.D: EO: rotor motion without ground translation; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate; IR: a concentrated hotspot beneath the rotor mast marks active engine exhaust

qa.question: Which cue pairing continues through camera/view change?

options.A: the object: the tarmac background; screen scale or angle changes
options.B: the object: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; screen scale or angle changes
options.C: the object: rotor motion without ground translation; screen scale or angle changes
options.D: the object: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; screen scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate

qa.question: Which EO cue is aligned with the IR contrast cue?

options.A: EO cue: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; IR cue: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.B: EO cue: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; IR cue: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.C: EO cue: rotor motion without ground translation; IR cue: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.D: EO cue: the tarmac background; IR cue: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate; IR: a concentrated hotspot beneath the rotor mast marks active engine exhaust

qa.question: Which competing cue appears in the cross-modal chain?

options.A: EO/visible cue: the tarmac background; IR/contrast context: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.B: EO/visible cue: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; IR/contrast context: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.C: EO/visible cue: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; IR/contrast context: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.D: EO/visible cue: rotor motion without ground translation; IR/contrast context: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast

qa.evidence_note: treating rotor activity as taxiing or relocation

qa.question: When the view changes, which cue is checked against the nearby alternative?

options.A: the tarmac background; rotor motion without ground translation
options.B: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; rotor motion without ground translation
options.C: rotor motion without ground translation; the tarmac background
options.D: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; the tarmac background

qa.evidence_note: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate

qa.question: Which IR cue pairing appears in this interval?

options.A: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; the tarmac background
options.B: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry
options.C: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; rotor motion without ground translation
options.D: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast; runway, taxiway, or apron markings

qa.evidence_note: a concentrated hotspot beneath the rotor mast marks active engine exhaust

qa.question: Which combined EO/IR sequence account is visible?

options.A: EO: rotor motion without ground translation; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.B: EO: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.C: EO: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast
options.D: EO: the tarmac background; IR: engine, pavement, or body thermal contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: main rotor blur is visible while the helicopter does not translate; IR: a concentrated hotspot beneath the rotor mast marks active engine exhaust

qa.question: For the full clip, which cue chain is visible?

options.A: the object: the tarmac background; nearby cue: rotor motion without ground translation
options.B: the object: apron, stand, grass, or pavement geometry; nearby cue: rotor motion without ground translation
options.C: the object: rotor motion without ground translation; nearby cue: the tarmac background
options.D: the object: runway, taxiway, or apron markings; nearby cue: the tarmac background

qa.evidence_note: treating rotor activity as taxiing or relocation

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
